justification: All 7 business rules identified with correct invariant classifications. Notification rule (BR-7) correctly marked invariant: false with clear justification. Sanctions rule (BR-5) included as invariant: true. Timezone ambiguity for daily limit reset explicitly surfaced as both an edge case and a formal escalation with options. 14 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided (well above the 4 minimum). Confidence level of 72 falls within the 60-85 range with detailed assumptions listed.

observations.text: All 7 rules identified and correctly classified — regulatory invariants (BR-1, BR-4, BR-5, BR-6) as true, policy/operational rules (BR-2, BR-3, BR-7) as false
Notification rule explicitly marked invariant: false with strong justification distinguishing operational/UX policy from data integrity constraints
Sanctions screening rule included as invariant: true with appropriate severity language about criminal liability
Timezone ambiguity surfaced prominently — both as an edge case and as a formal escalation with three concrete options and a recommendation
14 acceptance criteria with well-structured Given/When/Then format, covering all major rules including boundary conditions
Correctly distinguishes partially-verified ($500) from unverified (receive-only) limits — no conflation
Daily limit amounts (BR-2, BR-3) correctly marked invariant: false — recognizing the existence of limits is invariant but the specific amounts are soft config
Excellent edge cases including race conditions on daily limits, mid-day KYC upgrade, and post-initiation sanctions status change
AC-14 explicitly tests the $1,000 boundary condition (exactly $1,000 does not trigger notification), showing careful spec reading
Escalations are well-structured with options and recommendations, going beyond minimum requirements
Identifies additional ambiguities beyond timezone: partially-verified withdrawal rights, dispute/chargeback process gap, and the $1,000 boundary condition
